mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly job gains above 100k signal robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 100k monthly gains.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production above 0.5% indicates strengthening manufacturing activity, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in 50%+ of periods when INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5% monthly.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth rate above 2% signals strong aggregate demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in 50%+ of periods when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5%) combined with low policy rates (FEDFUNDS < 1%) creates stagflation risks that depress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS < 1%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in unemployment (UNRATE falls > 0.3pp in 3 months) signals tight labor markets that may trigger inflationary pressures, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE declines > 0.3pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in housing starts (HOUST < 600k) indicates weak demand for construction, which correlates with negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where HOUST falls below 600k.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2%) and tightening monetary policy (FEDFUNDS > 4%) creates a hostile environment for equities, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL > 2% and FEDFUNDS > 4%.
